title: [phase:simulate] [family:canary-migration] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Simulate a canary-ready migration path from openclaw-tmux to the wrapper prototype. Return concise bullets: first canary workflow, stop conditions, rollback triggers, manifest/doc migration orde...
summary_text: - Push canary will now propagate binary selection end-to-end on the NotebookLM smoke workflow: `vars.FUGUE_NOTEBOOKLM_BIN` feeds both `NOTEBOOKLM_READINESS_NLM_BIN` (readiness gate) and `NLM_BIN` (preflight), so the new resolver should remove plain `command -v nlm` path misses. - On push trigger, context is force-seeded to NotebookLM visual brief (`content_hint_applied=true`, action/skill=`notebooklm-visual-brief`), so the `notebooklm-runtime` job should run rather than skip. - Readiness gate behavior will be strict-live (`LIVE_SMOKE_MODE=required`, `EXECUTE_LIVE_MODE=required`) but still `SKIP_REPO_TESTS=true`, so it validates runtime capability, not full repo test coverage. - Most likely next blocker after path resolution: optional-skill mismatch. Workflow sync installs optional skills only when hint includes `slide-prep`, but readiness gate always sets `NOTEBOOKLM_READINESS_REQUIRE_OPTIONAL=true`; on a runner missing `~/.codex/skills/notebooklm-slide-prep` or `~/.claude/skills/notebooklm-slide-prep`, it should fail before live execution. If those are already present, next likely blocker is `nlm` auth/runtime smoke failure. - Recommended follow-up check: add a pre-readiness runner assertion for optional skill presence (`notebooklm-slide-prep` in both Codex/Claude skill dirs) to confirm this blocker before rerunning canary.
